patch.value:
## Context
Every existing closed item (511 closed Tasks alone; effectively 100% of terminal items across types, verified 2026-07-07) predates a working `closed_at` stamp (defect pm-m4iu). Until they carry `closed_at`, any metadata write on them re-buckets the item into `[Unreleased]` via the `updated_at` fallback — which is exactly what blocks pm-e9zh (close_reason:18 + resolution backfill) and pm-pivf (GH-209 duplicate issue-code rename).

## Derivation (deterministic, no placeholders)
The hash-chained history streams (`.agents/pm/history/<id>.jsonl`) record every mutation as a timestamped JSON-patch op. For each closed item missing `closed_at`:
- `closed_at := ts` of the earliest history entry whose patch puts `status` at closed with no later transition away from closed; for items created already-closed (e.g. pm-2lbp, created 2026-05-01 with closed status), that is the `create` op itself.
- Items whose history is incomplete (pre-history-era or repaired chains) go to a documented residual list instead of receiving a guessed value.

## Delivery
Prefer extending `pm validate --fix-hints` with a closed_at hint plus a new `--auto-fix --fix-scope timestamps` remediation (matches the existing fix-hint machinery; today's hints cover close_reason/title/resolution only). A `pm run normalize` rule is the fallback home. The backfill write is an ordinary history-recorded mutation, so the immutable-proof chain stays intact; the assigned value must equal what history replay yields.

## Ordering
Run only after pm-m4iu ships (otherwise newly closed items regress immediately) and regenerate + review the changelog in the same commit as the first bulk pass. Once complete, pm-e9zh and pm-pivf become executable without `[Unreleased]` churn.
Deterministic closed_at backfill for legacy closed items derived from history streams
Backfill closed_at on all legacy terminal items from their history streams so closed items stop bucketing by updated_at.

validate emits a fix hint for closed items missing closed_at; auto-fix (new timestamps fix-scope) populates closed_at solely from history-replay evidence and is idempotent; items with unrecoverable history land on a documented residual list with counts; changelog regenerated in the same commit as the bulk pass shows zero unintended [Unreleased] churn

author: maintainer-agent
message: Drop redundant related edges; the reverse blocked_by edges on pm-e9zh/pm-pivf carry the relationship and the pair formed a dependency cycle
